title: ICT Specialist 2 (800351) ICT Specialist 2 (800351) ICT Specialist 2 (800351)
organization: International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC)
description:
What we do
 
The International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC) works worldwide to provide protection and humanitarian assistance to people affected by conflict and armed violence. We take action in response to emergencies and, at the same time, promote respect for international humanitarian law. We are an independent and neutral organization, and our mandate stems essentially from the Geneva Conventions of 1949. We work closely with National Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies and with their International Federation in order to ensure a concerted, rational and rapid humanitarian response to the needs of the victims of armed conflict or any other situation of internal violence. We direct and coordinate the international activities conducted in these situations.
 Purpose
 
ICT Specialist 2 manages and organizes all ICT systems and services in the location where s/he is posted. S/he assists the ICT Manager in designing and installing ICT infrastructure.
 Relationships
Internally, interacts with ICRC employees.Externally, interacts with local suppliers.Accountabilities & functional responsibilities

Provide support for Level 2 ICT Service Requests and ensure compliance with policies that govern the Global ICT Support Model framework.
Enforces the use of the ICRC service portal for ICT requests and incident reporting
Under the technical supervision of the ICT Coordinator, participate in planning, deployment, and maintenance of ICT systems such as PABX, data networks, Radio systems (VFH&HF), VSAT, etc.
Installs, maintains, and repairs IT hardware, infrastructure (LAN, SDWAN, wireless), and telecom (VHF, SAT phones, and UC) in line with ICRC standards.
Follow-up of ICT projects in the defined area of responsibilities, in coordination with the ICT Coordinator and other relevant departments.
Ensures existing ICT infrastructure is functional and performs regular quality control checks
Exploit and adapt existing systems, mainly independently.
Ensures that global and country-level policies and procedures are implemented and followed.
Participate in maintaining proper tracking of ICT equipment allocations, conduct regular physical checks, and monitor shipments to and from other ICRC sites.
Assist in ensuring that the inventory is accurate, comprehensive, and reliable, and that the ICT stocks are adequately managed.
Collaborates with the ICT team, suppliers, and service providers to ensure that requested goods or services are delivered according to the established standards.
Prepares for meetings by setting up rooms and materials, including technical set-up for conference calls, presentations, and video conferences.
Keeps technical documentation up to date for all IT installations.
Briefs new staff on standard computer systems (hardware and/or software), information management, information security and compliance with visual identity rules, correspondence protocol, etc.
Reports regularly or upon request to his superiors on the department’s activities, pending, state of projects, problems, etc. for the sites under his/her responsibility.
Other duties as assigned.
Certifications / Education required
 

University degree in Computer Science, Telecommunications, or related field from a recognized institution.
Professional certification in the domains of Network, Operating Systems, and Software 
ICT management certification (ITIL) is an asset

 Professional experience required

At least 4 years of troubleshooting experience in an area of the ICT service catalogue (e.g. workplace services, telecommunications, IT, help desk, etc.).
Proven technical troubleshooting and problem-solving skills
Experience in first-level support, LAN/WAN, Cyber Security, and Cloud computing.
Good knowledge in Telecommunications (VHF, HF, VSATs, PBX, Telephony, etc.)
Experience in MS Teams environment, Project Management, and cybersecurity
High degree of flexibility and responsiveness.
Very good language skills in English and Amharic
A driving license with experience is a big plus

 Additional information

Type of role: National / Resident (Opened to Ethiopian Nationals ONLY) 
Working rate: 100%
Location: Addis Ababa 
Job level: B2
Length of assignment: 1 year fixed term contract, subject to extension based on performance and budget availability
Hardship : H1 
Application deadline: 22/3/2026
Our values
At the ICRC, we value impact, collaboration, respect, and compassion. We seek candidates who demonstrate behaviors based on these shared values. For more information on the ICRC values, please visit this page.

title: Finance & Administration Site Manager 1 Finance & Administration Site Manager 1 Finance & Administration Site Manager 1
organization: International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC)
description:
What we do
 
The International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC) works worldwide to provide protection and humanitarian assistance to people affected by conflict and armed violence. We take action in response to emergencies and, at the same time, promote respect for international humanitarian law. We are an independent and neutral organization, and our mandate stems essentially from the Geneva Conventions of 1949. We work closely with National Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies and with their International Federation in order to ensure a concerted, rational and rapid humanitarian response to the needs of the victims of armed conflict or any other situation of internal violence. We direct and coordinate the international activities conducted in these situations.
 Purpose
 
Finance & Administration Site Manager 1 is accountable for the integrity of financial and analytical accounting and for reporting from the field/offices. S/he oversees all financial resources and administrative activities, such as management of premises and staff travel, in a given delegation/other structure, and acts as Human Resources and/or Logistics Manager in the absence of a dedicated specialist. S/he has responsibilities within the assigned site concerning financial business partnering, risk management, financial compliance, resource management and quality administrative services
 Relationships
 

Internally, interacts with all relevant colleagues in HAW, NEK and ADD and other sub delegation of the ICRC as needed.
Externally, s/he interacts with a network of contacts at local level such as bank representatives, suppliers and service providers, under the direct supervision of the Head of Office Hawassa and the technical support of the NEK Sub-Delegation.

 Accountabilities & functional responsibilities
 
Administration and Finance (35%)
 

Finance

 

Ensures accurate, complete, and timely accounting records and financial reports in line with ICRC financial procedures
Manages office/site-level cash operations, including petty cash, payments, advances, reconciliations, and cash-flow monitoring
Prepares and follows up on monthly financial closings in coordination with the delegation finance structure
Records accounting transactions in the designated financial systems and ensures supporting documentation is complete
Identifies financial discrepancies, risks, or anomalies and proposes corrective actions
Maintains audit-ready financial and administrative documentation (physical and electronic, including Team Space)
Provides administrative and financial support to departments as required
Provides functional guidance to administrative or finance support staff, where applicable. 

 
Premises and General Services
 

Oversees the management, maintenance, and safety of office premises, residences, and other assigned facilities
Coordinates maintenance, rehabilitation, or construction works in line with ICRC standards and budgets
Manages relationships with landlords and external service providers
Monitors utilities and general services consumption and proposes efficiency measures
Manages inventories related to premises and ensures proper documentation and stock control.
Welcome and Office Support
 

Organizes accommodation and logistical arrangements for visitors and staff on mission
Prepares welcome briefings and supports induction processes for new arrivals
Supports the organization of meetings, workshops, and events.
2. Logistics Responsibilities (30%)
 
Fleet and Transport Management
 

Ensures efficient and compliant planning, allocation, and use of vehicles
Oversees fuel management and monitors consumption, reporting anomalies
Monitors vehicle condition and coordinates preventive and corrective maintenance
Ensures compliance with fleet management and safety rules.

 
Transport Staff Supervision
 

Organizes transport staff assignments and monitors task execution
Conducts briefings and debriefings related to movements, safety, and operational feedback
Ensures compliance with safety and operational rules by drivers and users.

 
Logistics Administration
 

Ensures fleet management tools, vehicle documentation, and records are accurate and up to date
Produces logistics statistics and reports as required
Ensures proper archiving of logistics documentation in line with ICRC rules.

 
Procurement and Supply
 

Manages procurement processes in compliance with ICRC procurement rules
Prepares and follows up on purchase orders, contracts, and supporting documentation
Conducts market assessments for local purchases to ensure best value for money
Coordinates delivery, reception, and payment processes
Ensures quality and quantity control upon receipt of goods and services
Handles supplier complaints in coordination with relevant stakeholders.
3. Human Resources – HR Relay (15%)
 

Acts as HR Relay at office/site level under the guidance of the delegation HR department
Ensures HR administrative processes are implemented in line with ICRC policies and l
